'''Josh White''' is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s. He played for [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]] and the [[Illawarra Steelers]] in the ARL competition. He also played for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]]. He is the younger brother of former rugby league player [[Kyle White (rugby league)|Kyle White]].

==Playing career==
White started his rugby league career at the Canterbury Bulldogs as a 17 year old and played from 1989 - 1991. White made his first grade debut in round 1 of the 1993 NSWRL season against the [[Gold Coast Seagulls]] at [[Campbelltown Sports Stadium]]. White spent five seasons at Western Suburbs before joining Illawarra in 1996. White only spent one year at Illawarra making 14 appearances. White then signed for the London Broncos in the Super League and enjoyed a successful season as the club finished runners up in the competition and receiving the Super League Player of the Month in April 1997. In 1998, White signed for Salford and finished as the clubs joint top try scorer, however they would finish second last on the table.<ref>Alan Whiticker/Glen Hudson: The Encyclopedia of Rugby League Players. (1995 edition) {{ISBN|1875169571}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.westsmagpies.com.au/the-archives/first-grade-players/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170721071203/http://www.westsmagpies.com.au/the-archives/first-grade-players/|url-status=dead|archive-date=21 July 2017|title=Western Suburbs first grade players|website=www.westsmagpies.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/the-mole-tommy-raudonikis-western-suburbs-magpies-tribute/29f78e37-e89b-4f47-87d7-30eba0d87c86/|title=The Mole: Former Wests Magpies player Josh White reveals Tommy Raudonikis initiation ritual|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/kyle-white-personal-problems-brothers-frantic-search/850cc0db-8721-4368-9570-666b2efe9211/|title=Former first-grader Kyle White hits rock bottom, sparking brother's frantic search|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>
